highlights.title: One unified Library
highlights.description: Web articles, ebooks, PDFs, WeRead items, and collections now live in one Library, with filters and paging that make it easier to return to the same view.

highlights.title: Organize with collections
highlights.description: Group related reading into a collection, then keep paging, filtering, and opening articles inside a focused Library subset.

highlights.title: Review inside the draft
highlights.description: The review assistant streams structured suggestions, previews additions, removals, and edits in the draft, and keeps organized discussion near the writing area.

highlights.title: Sync and updates need less work
highlights.description: WeRead can sync automatically, and Yomitomo now checks for updates silently with a persistent top-bar entry when a new version is available.

highlights.title: More stable over time
highlights.description: SQLite storage, Chromium cache, and log retention are tighter, with fixes for PDF highlight clicks, web translation selection, failed assistant states, and ebook titles.

highlights.title: Anonymous stats can be disabled
highlights.description: Settings now includes an anonymous version and system metrics switch. It helps understand active version and system distribution without collecting reading content, titles, annotations, file paths, or AI chats.
